content.summary: Using CHOP Export or expressions to drive TOP parameters (like transform, level, blur) from CHOP channels creates responsive, data-driven visuals. This is a fundamental TD pattern for audio-reactive and sensor-driven work.
warnings: CHOP exports override parameter expressions — you cannot have both active simultaneously
High-frequency CHOP data may cause excessive cooking if exported to many TOPs

recipe.description: Create a CHOP chain that produces a 0-1 channel, then reference it from a TOP parameter expression.

example.description: Expression on a TOP parameter to read a CHOP channel value

steps: Create Audio Device CHOP or LFO CHOP as data source
Add Math CHOP to normalize range to 0-1
On the target TOP, set parameter expression to op('math1')['chan1']
Or use CHOP Export: right-click Math CHOP output → Export CHOP
